sail_ingest.post_fix_notes: After the 8 integer/control opcodes were added, every instruction along the natural dynamic path decodes and executes without abort. v_cmpx_gt_u64_e64 evaluates 0 > 0 for unset kernargs, driving EXEC to 0; s_cbranch_execz at PC 0x144 then branches over the entire loop body straight to s_endpgm at PC 0x1E4. The FP multiply (v_mul_f32_e32) inside the loop is therefore never reached in this default-state run — exercising it requires kernarg priming. No remaining integer/control gaps in this sample.

coverage_instances.note: Static coverage — counts opcodes that have a Sail AST entry. Only v_mul_f32_e32 remains unsupported. Dynamic execution traverses 14 of 40 instructions (the prologue plus s_cbranch_execz over the loop) and reaches s_endpgm; the FP multiply is in the loop body that EXEC=0 skips.

unsupported_opcodes.notes: No floating-point arithmetic in spec — this is the kernel's core operation

blocking_summary.execution_coverage_note: 14 of 40 static instructions executed (35%). The kernel reaches s_endpgm without any unsupported-opcode abort because the loop body is branched-around when EXEC=0. v_mul_f32_e32 is never dynamically reached in this run — would require kernarg priming to drive EXEC non-zero through the loop.
blocking_summary.fundamental_gap: No FP arithmetic in Sail. v_mul_f32 is the kernel's actual work; reaching it dynamically requires kernarg setup, but the opcode itself would still abort.
